core_loop: Muster→March→7-phase Battle Turn (Strategy Declaration→Volley→Manoeuvre→Offensive Thread→Engagement→Cascade→Reform, repeated) →Part D aftermath scene + named-officer fallout→Part E faction/settlement consequences→between-battle reinforcement/Discipline persistence.

decision_points.where: Phase 1 Strategy Declaration (formation×split×tactic×Thread intent per sub-unit)
decision_points.options: 6 tactics with counters, 7 formations, Off/Def split, Thread intent
decision_points.note: real counter-web but tempered by Command-dominance axiom

decision_points.options: split across engagements vs concentrate fire
decision_points.note: doctrine's own cited win-rate evidence now suspect, see findings

decision_points.where: Phase 6 General action: Personal combat (General Duel, §3.7)
decision_points.note: real risk-reward but thin follow-through, no Thread option

decision_points.options: rear-safe vs frontline-risky Leap; Coherence spend toward Severance
Tend wounded / Survey damage / Address population
decision_points.note: strongest decision point in the lane, distinct payoffs incl. hidden Finding

decision_points.options: cheap defensive Levy vs costly mobile Professional under Campaign Supply attrition

decision_points.note: never hooked in mass_battle_v30.md's own turn structure

north_star.collision_feed: General Duel and the aftermath scene are the two designed personal↔strategic collision points (officer capture/disposition, settlement Order, hidden Findings); both well-built but General Duel is Thread-blind and Scene→Mass is unconsumed, narrowing the actual feed below what's designed.
north_star.verdict: Points at the North Star strongly in its strategic/consequence layer (aftermath scene, campaign composition, faction/settlement fallout) but its turn-by-turn tactical layer is narrower than its apparent complexity once Command-dominance and contaminated splitting evidence are accounted for.

nq.observation: §A.1's explicit axiom "Command=7 vs Command=1 produces a near-certain outcome before a die is rolled" is DELIBERATE but tensions with the three-viable-approaches test and Ω non-dominance.

ED-1019+ per-sub-unit continuous-Morale/aggregate/cascade model is deep; the Army Morale composite is the only legible player-facing surface over it — depth-vs-legibility gap worth checking for HUD parity.
Campaign Supply / Levy Restriction model real historical military-political dynamics with one-sentence rules and real strategic bite — solid N grounding.

threadwork_junctures.juncture: §A.10 Thread ops in mass battle (Ob/Coherence-cost table by scale)
General Duel (§3.7) practitioner-general Leap option during personal-combat exchange
threadwork_junctures.evidence: scale_transitions_v30.md §3.7 has no Thread option; inherits combat_engine_v1's zero-threadwork state, ED-911 (open, P1)
scale_transitions_v30.md §3.8 covers Social/Investigation/Combat wins only, no Thread-scene win, despite Thread being the densest personal-scale action in §A.10

legibility.note: Whose-position-at-risk: yes, via Army Morale composite + persistent Discipline. What-happens-if-no-one-acts: yes, via Campaign Supply/Prosperity-0 attrition/idle-army Morale loss (all explicit). What-each-actor-wants: no — no motive/goal field exists in this subsystem's own state; imported from faction/NPC layers.

edges.mechanism: +1D/free Reform, 1-turn duration; no hook in mass_battle_v30.md
terrain polygon query at battle coords
leading-canon numeric authority; geometry (§A.3b) and splitting-doctrine numbers flagged superseded/contaminated

directions.top-down: The Command-dominance axiom is philosophy-consistent with "generalship is the battle" but strains Ω non-dominance as literally written.
directions.bottom-up: The sim engine (config.py/orchestration.py) is leading canon and has moved past this doc's geometry/pool formulas in several banner-flagged places; ED-MB-0002 (2026-07-04) shows the doc's own cited splitting-doctrine simulation evidence may be contaminated by an invincibility-bug baseline.
directions.lateral: General Duel and Scene→Mass are the sibling-subsystem hooks into mass battle; both are thinner in practice than mass battle's own rich Thread integration.
directions.diagonal: The aftermath scene plus named-officer system is the strongest cross-scale narrative payoff mechanism in this lane.
directions.forwards: LC-8/ED-MB-0001/ED-MB-0002 show active, disciplined engine work in flight; design-doc battlefield geometry (§A.3b) is explicitly marked superseded pending LC-8 re-derivation.
directions.backwards: §A.3b geometry and §A.8 splitting-doctrine numbers both carry acknowledged supersession/contamination risk already flagged in-repo.

findings.claim: The General Duel (§3.7), the single richest personal-scale collision point in mass battle, has no Thread/Leap option for a practitioner-general and so inherits combat_engine_v1's zero-threadwork state.

The §3.8 Scene→Mass bonus table (Social/Investigation/Combat win modifiers) is declared in scale_transitions_v30.md but never referenced or hooked anywhere in mass_battle_v30.md's own 7-phase turn structure.

§A.8's splitting-dominates-concentration doctrine cites simulation win-rate evidence (+9% to +45%) of exactly the kind ED-MB-0002's RC-2 shows was measured against a fictitious invincibility-bug baseline (frozen multi-subunit cohesion under PER_CELL=0), so the design doc's own stated rationale for discouraging concentration may rest on contaminated numbers.
findings.tracked_ref: ED-MB-0002 (new scope: implicates this doc's own cited evidence, not just the engine)

findings.claim: The stated design axiom "Command=7 vs Command=1 produces a near-certain outcome before a die is rolled" is DELIBERATE but sits in direct tension with the charter's Ω non-dominance clause and the Q-robust three-viable-approaches test.

From mass_battle_v30.md/integration_v30.md state alone, there is no field for a named general's or faction's goal/motive, so the dramatic-legibility question "what does each named actor want" cannot be answered from this subsystem in isolation.
findings.cite: mass_battle_v30.md (whole doc, no motive/goal field); scale_transitions_v30.md §3.7-§3.8

findings.claim: Even where consumed, the §3.8 Scene→Mass bonus (+1D one unit, 1 turn, or a free Reform) is a small, single-turn modifier that is mechanically negligible against the Command-dominance axiom it would need to offset, undercutting the narrative payoff of winning the antecedent scene.

Mass battle's own Thread integration (§A.10) is unusually mature (full scale-tiered Ob/Coherence table, positioning rules) while the personal-combat layer it hands off to for General Duel is Thread-blind, an asymmetry that compounds ED-911's severity rather than being a separate defect.

The ED-1019+ per-sub-unit continuous (float) Morale/aggregate/cascade model is mechanically deep but has no player-facing surface besides the single Army Morale composite; if the videogame HUD doesn't expose per-sub-unit state, the depth is invisible and if it does, the composite's legibility value is undercut by exposing raw floats.
